witnesses.description: Two finite functions and their composite.
A finite function with a concrete image and preimage query.
A finite bijection and its inverse table.
Three finite functions for composition associativity.
A total function that cannot have an inverse because two inputs share one output.

checks.claim: The listed composite table equals g after f.
checks.notes: The validator checks both input functions and recomputes every composite graph entry.

checks.claim: The listed image and preimage match the finite function table.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the image and preimage sets exactly.

checks.claim: The listed inverse table is the inverse of a finite bijection.
checks.notes: The validator checks bijectivity, inverse graph entries, and both identity compositions.

checks.claim: The two listed finite composition-associativity tables agree.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes g o f, h o g, h o (g o f), and (h o g) o f.

checks.claim: The listed non-injective function has a two-sided inverse.
checks.notes: The validator checks the function is total but not injective, with the listed collision.

checks.claim: Function extensionality, inverse laws, and categorical composition laws over arbitrary types require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: function extensionality, inverse laws, image/preimage laws, and associativity of composition for arbitrary types
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite table rows are checked; general function theory remains proof-assistant territory.
